page: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor architecture is x86_64, supported by a 32 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the top 25%, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches top-tier performance in the top 10%. However, multi-threaded scaling is a notable tradeoff; multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falls into the bottom 25%, and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is average. Memory bandwidth is stable, averaging 372.64 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 70.47 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database and web serving operations perform at average levels, with Redis SET operations handling 6.88 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 12.55 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance. The high memory density relative to the virtual core count makes it well-suited for caching layers, medium-sized database deployments, and memory-bound utility scripts. It can also handle lightweight LLM inference for small models, though performance drops significantly on larger models. It is less suitable for sustained, multi-threaded compute-heavy workloads due to its shared CPU architecture and lower multi-threaded benchmark scaling.
description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC x86_64 platform with 32.0 GB of RAM and 240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with single-threaded bzip3 decompression in the top 10% and single-core stress-ng in the top 25%. However, multi-threaded scaling is limited, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falling into the bottom 25%. This resource profile provides a cost-effective solution for memory-intensive but CPU-burstable workloads. It is highly suitable for database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose web serving, but less optimal for sustained, multi-threaded compute tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 240 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 240 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage, balanced for memory-intensive and burstable workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory
240 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core performance and single-threaded decompression speeds
Supports AVX-512 and hardware-accelerated cryptography instructions
Optimized for memory-bound workloads, caching, and database operations
